title: calceolaria ascendens subsp. glandulifera
intro: Calceolaria ascendens subsp. glandulifera, also known as the 'Pocketbook Plant', is a flowering plant species belonging to the Calceolaria genus, characterized by its unique pocket-like shape and yellow to orange flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Calceolaria ascendens subsp. glandulifera include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
